title: रीढ़ की हड्डी – Concept Mastery Helper
author: जगदीश चन्द्र माथुर

concepts.lesson: The play shows how marriage is often treated like a business deal where the girl is judged and evaluated instead of respected as a person.
concepts.real_example: In many arranged marriage meetings, families judge the girl based on appearance, education, or behaviour.
key_points: Girl is examined by the groom’s family

concepts.lesson: Uma is confident, educated, and courageous. She refuses to remain silent when she realizes she is being treated like an object.
concepts.real_example: Educated individuals standing up against unfair traditions.

concepts.lesson: Ram Swarup represents a father caught between social pressure and personal values. He wants a good marriage for his daughter but hides her education to satisfy society.
concepts.real_example: Parents sometimes hide or change facts to meet social expectations.

concepts.lesson: The play exposes the hypocrisy of society where people claim to value education but reject educated girls for marriage.
concepts.real_example: Families who want educated sons but prefer less educated daughters-in-law.

concepts.lesson: The title 'Reedh Ki Haddi' (Backbone) symbolises courage and moral strength. Uma’s bold stand represents the backbone that many people lack.
concepts.real_example: A person who stands up for truth is said to have a strong backbone.

concepts.concept_name: Conflict Between Tradition and Modern Thinking
concepts.lesson: The play highlights the clash between traditional arranged marriage expectations and modern educated values represented by Uma.
concepts.real_example: Young people today questioning outdated social customs.

concepts.lesson: Uma demonstrates the courage to speak truth even when it might affect her marriage prospects. This shows the importance of self-respect over social approval.
concepts.real_example: People refusing unfair job interviews or discriminatory treatment.

concepts.lesson: The play sends a strong message that society must respect women's education, dignity, and independence instead of judging them by outdated standards.
concepts.real_example: Encouraging equal opportunities for women in education and careers.
